name: x4-forge-studio
description: Visual modding studio for X4: Foundations â€” build, validate, and deploy mods with a node canvas and real-schema validation, no hand-written XML required.

viewsWelcome.contents:
Build, validate, and deploy X4: Foundations mods on a visual canvas.

[Open Studio](command:x4forge.openStudio)

Opens the full studio in an editor tab, starting the local backend automatically.

[Create Agent Key](command:x4forge.createAgentKey)
[Show Backend Logs](command:x4forge.showLogs)
[Stop Backend](command:x4forge.stopSidecar)

x4forge.attachUrl.description: URL of an already-running Forge to attach to before spawning a sidecar. The extension attaches only if this URL answers the Forge agent-schema probe; it never stops a server it attached to.

x4forge.forgeRoot.description: Optional path to a built Forge checkout (must contain dist/server.cjs and node_modules). When empty, the app bundled inside the extension is used.

x4forge.stateDir.description: Directory for the sidecar's workspace state (X4_STATE_DIR). When empty, a per-user directory in the extension's global storage is used. Point it at an existing Forge checkout's .studio-state to open the real workspace â€” do NOT do this while that Forge's own server is running (the attach probe normally prevents this).

x4forge.nodePath.description: Path to the Node.js executable used to run the sidecar. When empty, 'node' from PATH is used. The sidecar requires a real Node install (native modules are ABI-matched to it).

x4forge.autoOpen.description: Open the X4 Forge Studio automatically when a (trusted) workspace finishes loading. Useful for dedicated modding workspaces; off by default.

enumDescriptions: Launch the backend with --inspect and auto-attach the Node debugger (breakpoints, stepping). Works in VS Code and Antigravity.
Like inspect, but pause at the first line so you can debug startup; continue execution in the debugger to start the server.
x4forge.debug.description: Attach a Node debugger to the managed backend sidecar. For SOURCE-LEVEL (TypeScript) breakpoints, also set x4forge.forgeRoot to a built repo checkout (its bundle keeps sourcemaps); against the bundled app you debug at the bundled-JS level. Default off.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: X4 Forge compiles, validates, and packages mod projects (filesystem writes and local process execution). The extension is disabled in untrusted workspaces.

virtualWorkspaces.description: The Forge backend requires a local filesystem.
